behavior_summary: This script implements the in-game behavior for a GOLEM item/NPC: a minimal U8P_GOLEM::look/inspect handler and a richer 'U8P_GOLEM::use' (command) handler that lets the player order the golem to Follow, Stop, Move, Open, Attack, Leave, Grunt, or do Nothing.
U8P_GOLEM::look: when the player inspects the golem it emits a short bark/label 'golem' and the U8P_GOLEM::look process yields.

U8P_GOLEM::use: constructs a player command menu, suspends to capture the choice, and dispatches per-command behavior. Several commands ask the player to pick a target (Follow/Move/Open/Attack) and then issue pathfinding/movement spawns, set combat targets, manipulate door items, clear follow references, or destroy the golem.

U8P_GOLEM::func03CC: a reusable approach/follow utility. When the primary actor is beyond a small range threshold it computes the direction to that actor and spawns preparatory and movement/animation processes so the golem approaches.
notable_control_flow: U8P_GOLEM::use performs an early guard: it queries combat state (Npc::isInCombat and Npc::getTarget) and may suppress or alter normal menu behavior when the golem is engaged in combat.
Menu flow uses Ask + SuspendAssign to capture the player's choice (local3 -> Ask, local4 <- SuspendAssign), then a strcmp-based branch selects the command handling block.

Commands that require targets (Follow, Move, Open, Attack) call Item/Ask target prompts and suspend while the player selects a target; after selection the script issues pathfind/spawn calls and may loop issuing Move attempts while incrementing a small counter to limit retries.

Open flow validates chosen object's type (Item::getType) and, for door-like objects, calls DOOR_NS::func05E4, clears a quality bit on the door and its linked partner, then spawns DOOR_NS::useDoor after pathing and animation.
Leave issues a leave animation/spawn and then calls Item::destroy on the golem instance to remove it.
open_questions: Exactly what the spawned methods (METHOD::func091C and METHOD::func15E7) do at runtime — they appear to prepare path/movement and then request movement/animation, but the precise semantics and parameters remain inferred.
The meaning and exact bit semantics of the quality flag cleared (~0x0400) when opening doors: inferred to affect blocking or open state but unconfirmed.

Whether the early combat guard fully disables the menu or only alters available options in specific combat sub-states (the structure shows an early test but exact resulting behavior isn't fully explicit).
Exact text of the menu options (local3) beyond the inferred labels and any locale variations.

main_function.name: U8P_GOLEM::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description/bark when the player examines the golem; display 'golem' and yield.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the player 'looks at' or inspects the GOLEM item (U8P_GOLEM::look/inspect event).

function_map.name: U8P_GOLEM::look
function_map.role: inspect/U8P_GOLEM::look handler that shows a short bark/label for the golem

function_map.name: U8P_GOLEM::use
function_map.role: player 'U8P_GOLEM::use' handler implementing a command menu (Follow/Stop/Move/Open/Attack/Leave/Grunt/Nothing) and dispatching movement, pathfinding, door interaction, combat target setting, and self-destruction

function_map.name: U8P_GOLEM::func03CC
function_map.role: utility to approach the primary actor: check range, compute direction, spawn preparatory and movement processes

internal_calls.purpose: Delegates Follow/approach behavior to a shared utility that computes direction and spawns movement processes so the golem approaches the primary actor

external_calls.purpose: Check whether the golem is currently in combat to gate or modify the U8P_GOLEM::use/menu interaction
Retrieve the golem's current combat target to compare it (for combat-related gating or behavior)
Measure distance to the primary actor to decide whether to initiate approach movement (threshold ~0x20)
Compute direction from the golem to the primary actor for movement/animation spawns
Spawn a preparatory/pathfinding process before issuing movement; inferred to ready path/movement toward a direction
Spawn the actual movement/animation/action process for the golem toward the computed direction
Compute direction toward a chosen Move/Follow/target for giving movement spawns
Command the golem to pathfind to a selected target (used by Move and Open flows)
Detect whether a chosen object is a door-like/openable type for the Open flow
Lookup a paired/related door item for linked door state updates when opening
Read door quality/flags prior to clearing a specific bit (inferred to affect blocking/open state)
Clear a specific quality bit (~0x0400) on a door (and linked door) as part of Open flow
Spawn or invoke the door-opening U8P_GOLEM::use logic after pathing and animation
Clear a stored follow reference (ref id 0x06) to make the golem stop following (Stop command)
Assign a chosen actor as the golem's combat target (Attack command)
Destroy the golem instance after playing the Leave animation (Leave command)

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the in-game behavior for a GOLEM item/NPC: a minimal U8P_GOLEM::look/inspect handler and a richer 'U8P_GOLEM::use' (command) handler that lets the player order the golem to Follow, Stop, Move, Open, Attack, Leave, Grunt, or do Nothing.
